content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (i.e, a simple undirected connected graph with N−1N-1N−1 edges). KKK of its vertices are colored black, and the other vertices are white.

For every xxx from 111 to KKK, solve the following problem:


Find the expected number of connected components in the resulting graph, if you delete exactly xxx randomly chosen black vertices from the tree. Each subset of xxx vertices has an equal probability of being chosen.

Deleting a vertex means you remove it and any edge incident to it from the tree.
Print the answer modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Formally, it can be shown that the required expected value can be represented as a rational number PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​, such that QQQ is coprime to 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Under this condition, there will exist a unique integer RRR such that 0≤R<109+70 \leq R \lt 10^9 + 70≤R<109+7, and P≡Q×R(mod109+7)P \equiv Q\times R \pmod{10^9 + 7}P≡Q×R(mod109+7).

Print RRR.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the number of vertices of the tree and number of black vertices.
The second line contains KKK space-separated integers x1,x2,…,xKx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Kx1​,x2​,…,xK​: the labels of the black vertices.
The next N−1N - 1N−1 lines describe the edges. The iii-th of these N−1N - 1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, print KKK space-separated integers: the answers for x=1,2,3,…,Kx = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, Kx=1,2,3,…,K, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: 1≤xi≤N1 \leq x_i \leq N1≤xi​≤N, and all the xix_ixi​ are distinct.
The input edges describe a tree on NNN vertices.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^{5}2⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Let's look at x=1x = 1x=1. There are 555 black vertices, each with an equal chance of being chosen.

If 111 is deleted, the number of components is 222.
If 222 is deleted, the number of components is 333.
If 555 is deleted, the number of components is 111.
If 666 is deleted, the number of components is 111.
If 777 is deleted, the number of components is 111.

The expected number of components is thus 15⋅(2+3+1+1+1)=85\frac{1}{5}\cdot (2+3+1+1+1) = \frac{8}{5}51​⋅(2+3+1+1+1)=58​.
Modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7, this is 200000003200000003200000003, because 200000003⋅5≡8(mod109+7)200000003 \cdot 5 \equiv 8 \pmod{10^9 + 7}200000003⋅5≡8(mod109+7)
The answers for the other components can be computed similarly.
Test case 222: All the black vertices are leaves, so no matter which subset of them is deleted, there'll be a single connected component. So, every answer is 111.
